Defendant Renewable Energy Trust Capital, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Stay The Action Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure §410.30
Off Calendar Apr-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel Laguardia, Esq. and Jonah Ross, Esq. of Shearman & Sterling LLP, T:415.616.1114, for defendant/moving party;
Ian E. Browning, Esq. and Bahram Seyedin-Noor, Esq of ALTO Litigation, T:415.868.5602, for plaintiff/opposing party;
Patricia Gorham, Esq., T:404.853.8298,and Robert P. Merten, III, Esq., T:916.241.0503, of Eversheds Sutherland LLP, by Court Call.

Prior to the hearing, Judge Kahn disclosed that Mr. Ross is the son of a colleague but this will not affect his ability to be fair & impartial in this case. No objections were raised

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT RENEWABLE ENERGY TRUST CAPITAL, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Stay The Action Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 410.30 as follows:

Defendant Renewable Energy Trust Capital, Inc.'s motion to dismiss is denied and its alternative motion to stay pending the conclusion of the New York lawsuit is granted. Because it is not clear that the New York lawsuit can or will resolve all the claims alleged in this action, dismissal of this lawsuit is unwarranted. However, application of forum non conveniens principles strongly supports staying this action pending the conclusion of the New York lawsuit. The New York Supreme Court ruled that New York has jurisdiction over PV2 and DERA and denied Mr. Pimentel's motion to dismiss for lack of jurisdiction, allowing jurisdictional discovery. Under these circumstances, New York is a suitable forum for all of the parties, with the at most arguably possible exception of Mr. Pimentel. Significantly, because Mr. Pimentel does not assert any claims of his own, he is not prejudiced by a stay of this case. Deference to a plaintiff's choice for forum is only one factor, and often not a dispositive one, in determining whether a stay should be granted on forum non conveniens grounds. Because of the forum selection provision in the Side Letter, New York, not California, is the only forum where all of the parties' disputes can be adjudicated, again with the possible exception of the claims against Mr. Pimentel. Forum non conveniens is an equitable doctrine and "[e]quity abhors a multiplicity of actions" (Simmons v. Superior Court (1950) 96 Cal.App.2d 119, 130) and thus California is an inconvenient forum for resolution of the parties' disputes. The public and private factors are tipped in favor of staying this case. The public interest in judicial economy and avoidance of possible inconsistent rulings strongly favor a stay. While California has a greater interest than New York in the development of a California renewable energy project, the parties' disputes do not concern the fate of the project. Rather, the parties' disputes arise out of financial transactions which are governed by New York, not California, law. The private factors of ease of access to proof and the cost and the availability of compulsory process for obtaining attendance of witnesses are equally balanced and do not favor either forum.

In addition, the Court allows plaintiffs to move for reconsideration of this Order without compliance with CCP 1008 as per Le Francois v. Goel.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT PANOCHE HOLDINGS LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Panoche Holdings, LLC's demurrer to the first through fifth and eighth causes of action in plaintiffs' amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all six causes of action. All six causes of action against Panoche Holdings seek rescission of the collateral transfer agreement. Yet, plaintiffs have not alleged the absence of a "complete and adequate remedy at law," which is a required element of a claim seeking rescission under New York law. (Loreley Financing (Jersey) No. 28. Ltd. v. Merill Lynch, Pierce, Fenner & Smith (N.Y. App. Div. 2014) 985 N.Y.S.2d 499, 504.) If they can do so in good faith, plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to cure this deficiency. For guidance of the parties going forward, the other grounds advanced by Panoche Holdings for its demurrer raise factual issues not amenable to resolution on a pleading motion. The allegations in the amended complaint do not necessarily show as a matter of law that plaintiffs acted insufficiently promptly in seeking rescission, rescission would unfairly prejudice Panoche Holdings or that the court is unable to restore the status quo ante. Whether the court may restore the status quo ante is a question of fact. (Lipsky v. Commonwealth United Corp. (2d Cir. 1976) 551 F.2d 887, 898.) Order signed. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Stay The Action Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 410.30 And 418.10
Off Calendar Mar-23-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ian E. Browning, of Alto Litigation, Tel. 415.766.8214, for plaintiffs;
Bahram Seyedin-Noor, of Alto Litigation, Tel. 415.868.5602, for plaintiffs;
John F. Cove, Jr., of Shearman & Sterling LLP, Tel. 415.616.1139, for defendants Renewable Energy Trust Capital, Inc.;
Alethea Sargent, of Shearman & Sterling LLP, Tel. 415.616.1264, for defendants Renewable Energy Trust Capital, Inc.;
Robert P. Merten, III, of Eversheds Sutherland (U.S.), LLP, Tel. 916.241.0503, for defendants Panoche Holdings, appearing via CourtCall;
Patricia Gorham, of Eversheds Sutherland (U.S.), LLP, Tel. 404.853.8298, for defendants Panoche Holdings, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court makes a personal disclosure, no objections are raised. The Court reaffirms its previous ruling. DEFENDANT RENEWABLE ENERGY TRUST CAPITAL, INC. Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Stay The Action Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 410.30 And 418.10 is denied. Assuming without deciding that both the Collateral Transfer Agreement and Side Letter are treated as a single contract and the forum selection clause in the Side Letter Agreement is not against the public policy of either the states of California or New York, the forum selection clause, by its own terms, only applies to a "dispute with respect to this letter agreement." Per both California and New York contract law, this court interprets the quoted language as mandating a New York forum only for claims that seek relief as to the Side Letter Agreement. Since none of the causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs PV2 Energy, LLC and DER Acquisition, LLC seek any relief as to the Side Letter Agreement, the forum selection clause in that agreement is inapplicable to those claims. While the court agrees that it is a poor idea for the parties to be litigating in both California and New York, RETC's argument for a stay on the grounds of forum nonconveniens based on the lawsuit it filed in New York was not well-developed in its moving papers and cannot be granted, at least at this time, due to RETC's failure to show either that New York is a suitable forum or California is an inconvenient forum. Moreover, RETC failed to adequately address the public and private factors that must be considered in ruling on a forum nonconveniens motion. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
